LAW AND MOTION 302, RESPONDENT RICHARD CURCI'S DEMURRER TO PETITIONER MARGARET REYES' FIRST AMENDED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS OVERRULED. MS. REYES HAS SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGED THAT THE TWO SUSPENSIONS WERE A MANIFEST ABUSE OF DISCRETION BASED ON HER ALLEGATIONS THAT THE SUSPENSIONS WERE IMPOSED IN RETALIATION FOR HER FILING A LAWSUIT AGAINST THE DISTRICT AND FOR ENGAGING IN WHISTLEBLOWER ACTIVITY EXPOSING THE PAR PROGRAM AS DISCRIMINATING ON THE BASIS OF AGE. MS. REYES ALSO SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGED THAT SHE WAS PREJUDICED IN PREPARING FOR HER DEFENSE AT THE SKELLY HEARINGS BECAUSE THE NOTICES GIVEN TO HER WERE PROCEDURALLY IMPROPER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE; HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; NOT REPORTED. =302/HK

DEMURRER TO AMENDED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Oct-10-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Margaret K. Reyes, Tel. No. 1-415-902-1883 Petitioner appearing in
pro-per;
No other appearances on the matter.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Respondent Richard Curci's demurrer to petitioner Margaret Reyes' first amended petition for writ of mandate is overruled. Ms. Reyes has sufficiently alleged that the two suspensions were a manifest abuse of discretion based on her allegations that the suspensions were imposed in retaliation for her filing a lawsuit against the District and for engaging in whistleblower activity exposing the PAR program as discriminating on the basis of age. Ms. Reyes also sufficiently alleged that she was prejudiced in preparing for her defense at the Skelly hearings because the notices given to her were procedurally improper. Order signed in open court. Judge; Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported. =302/hk

DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Jun-25-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Margaret Reyes 415-902-1883 Petitioner in Pro Per.
Jashoda Kashyap, Esq. and Eugene Elliot, Esq. 415-353-0999 x103 of Bertrand Fox Elliot Osman & Wenzel for Respondent Richard Cuci.

RULING - A R G U E D. Plaintiff argues 15 days and suspension. Defendant Argues. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

RESPONDENT RICHARD CURCI's DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE.

Sustained with leave to amend. The Courrt sustains the demurrer on the grounds that the holding of the hearing and the issuance and care carrying out of the discipline has mooted the petition. Petitioner has 60 days, until August 27, 2018 to allege a claim why the discipline was improperly imposed.

Respondent to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. =(302/HK)
